_comment: palette: keep in sync with anvil/lib/figures/palette.py and anvil/skills/deck/assets/anvil-deck.css :root. Passed to mmdc via `-c`. JSON has no comments, so this sync note lives in a _comment key; mermaid ignores unknown top-level keys. See anvil/skills/deck/assets/figure-conventions.md section 3.
_themeCSS_comment: themeCSS ships four canonical semantic classDefs (anvil-accent, anvil-muted, anvil-warning, anvil-success) injected verbatim into a <style> block in every rendered SVG. Authors apply via `:::anvil-warning` etc. on a node in their .mmd source. Each class triple-sets fill+stroke+text with !important so it overrides mermaid's per-source classDef defaults AND ensures the text is white-on-color (the white-on-white sub-symptom from heirloom/consent_flow). The selectors `.anvil-X > rect, .anvil-X > polygon` target the shape; `.anvil-X span` targets the foreignObject text. Hexes here MUST match ANVIL_NAVY/ANVIL_MUTED/ANVIL_WARNING/ANVIL_SUCCESS in palette.py — drift caught by tests/lib/test_figures_mermaid_classdefs.py.
themeCSS: .anvil-accent > rect, .anvil-accent > polygon { fill: #1f4e7a !important; stroke: #1a1a1a !important; } .anvil-accent span, .anvil-accent .nodeLabel { color: #ffffff !important; fill: #ffffff !important; } .anvil-muted > rect, .anvil-muted > polygon { fill: #6b6b6b !important; stroke: #1a1a1a !important; } .anvil-muted span, .anvil-muted .nodeLabel { color: #ffffff !important; fill: #ffffff !important; } .anvil-warning > rect, .anvil-warning > polygon { fill: #b5651d !important; stroke: #1a1a1a !important; } .anvil-warning span, .anvil-warning .nodeLabel { color: #ffffff !important; fill: #ffffff !important; } .anvil-success > rect, .anvil-success > polygon { fill: #2d5f3f !important; stroke: #1a1a1a !important; } .anvil-success span, .anvil-success .nodeLabel { color: #ffffff !important; fill: #ffffff !important; }
